evals.prompt: I need to write a proposal for adding a rate-limiting feature to our API. I have some rough notes but no structure yet.
evals.expected_behavior: Confirms document type and audience, proposes a section outline, and waits for agreement before drafting body content.
assertions: Confirms or asks about the intended audience
Proposes a section outline before writing body content
Does not front-load a full draft without outline confirmation

evals.prompt: Help me write an RFC for introducing a new caching layer. The audience is internal engineers who already know the current architecture.
evals.expected_behavior: Scopes the document type as RFC, acknowledges the known-audience context, and proposes an RFC-appropriate section structure (motivation, proposal, alternatives, drawbacks) before drafting.
assertions: Identifies the document type as RFC
Proposes a section structure appropriate for an RFC
Does not draft body sections before outline is confirmed

evals.prompt: Draft an ADR for our decision to switch from PostgreSQL to SQLite for local developer environments.
evals.expected_behavior: Scopes the document type as an ADR/decision doc, proposes standard ADR sections (context, decision, consequences/tradeoffs), and confirms the framing before filling in body content.
assertions: Identifies the document type as ADR or decision doc
Proposes sections covering context, decision, and consequences
Does not conflate the decision doc with a migration plan or implementation task list

evals.prompt: Write a migration guide for upgrading from v1 to v2 of our SDK.
evals.expected_behavior: Treats this as an audience-facing prose guide (what changed, how to update code, examples), not a task breakdown with owners and dependencies. Proposes guide sections before drafting.
assertions: Frames the output as prose guidance for SDK consumers, not a task plan
Does not produce an owner/dependency breakdown
Proposes section structure before writing body content

evals.prompt: We have a complete draft of an RFC. Do a final review pass before I send it out.
evals.expected_behavior: Recognizes that no draft was attached and asks the user to provide the RFC content before proceeding with a review pass.
assertions: Does not attempt a review pass without the actual draft content
Asks the user to share or paste the RFC draft
Does not fabricate or assume draft content

evals.prompt: This README is already written. Can you do a readability audit and tell me the Flesch score?
evals.expected_behavior: Defers to readability-check rather than handling the readability audit itself.
assertions: Recognizes this is a readability audit, not a drafting task
Suggests or defers to readability-check

evals.prompt: Is my npm package description too long? Does it fit within the npm character limit?
evals.expected_behavior: Defers to metadata-check rather than treating this as a document drafting request.
assertions: Recognizes this is a metadata string review, not document drafting
Suggests or defers to metadata-check

evals.prompt: I have a vague ask. Can you rewrite it into a sharper, more actionable prompt?
evals.expected_behavior: Defers to reverse-prompt rather than drafting a document.
assertions: Recognizes this is prompt sharpening, not document drafting
Suggests or defers to reverse-prompt

evals.prompt: Before we implement this feature, define the GOAL, CONSTRAINTS, FORMAT, and FAILURE conditions as a task contract.
evals.expected_behavior: Defers to prompt-contracts rather than drafting a document.
assertions: Recognizes this is a task contract definition, not document drafting
Suggests or defers to prompt-contracts

evals.prompt: Create a stable planner handoff artifact for this implementation so reviewers can approve the plan.
evals.expected_behavior: Defers to workflow-contracts rather than treating this as a document drafting task.
assertions: Recognizes this is a structured workflow handoff artifact, not a prose document
Suggests or defers to workflow-contracts

evals.prompt: Write a migration plan with owners, task dependencies, rollout phases, and acceptance criteria for the DB upgrade.
evals.expected_behavior: Recognizes this is a structured task plan, not a migration guide, and defers to plan-review.
assertions: Distinguishes between a migration plan (task breakdown) and a migration guide (audience-facing prose)
Suggests or defers to plan-review for the task breakdown
